source_pr.title: disttask: fix keep reporting error when cancel pending task
source_pr.body:

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->


symptoms: Task cancellation can happen before `updateTask`, leaving the disttask dispatcher in an inconsistent state (covered by `TestTaskCancelledBeforeUpdateTask`).
failure_signature: Task cancellation can happen before `updateTask`, leaving the disttask dispatcher in an inconsistent state (TestTaskCancelledBeforeUpdateTask).
root_cause_explanation: This case is flaky because task cancellation is an asynchronous state transition: the dispatcher can dispatch subtasks and then get cancelled before `updateTask(...)` persists the new task state. Depending on timing, the revert/error-handling path could observe `StepInit` (no real subtasks to revert) but still try to enumerate scheduler IDs and build revert subtasks, causing intermittent errors. The fix guards `dispatchSubTask4Revert` to skip revert-subtask creation when the task is still `StepInit`, and adds a failpoint-driven regression test (`TestTaskCancelledBeforeUpdateTask`) to deterministically exercise the cancel-before-update window.
fix_pattern: Make disttask state transitions resilient to cancellation timing: if the task is still `StepInit`, skip creating revert subtasks; add a failpoint + unit test to cover the cancel-before-update path and assert the dispatcher reaches a stable final state.

evidence: evidence: task cancellation can happen before `updateTask` (TestTaskCancelledBeforeUpdateTask)

analysis:
PR title: disttask: fix keep reporting error when cancel pending task
PR url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/48906

Triage summary: task cancellation can happen before `updateTask` (TestTaskCancelledBeforeUpdateTask)
Changed files (3): pkg/disttask/framework/BUILD.bazel, pkg/disttask/framework/dispatcher/dispatcher.go, pkg/disttask/framework/framework_test.go
Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
